title: Pass 5.F.1: README/lib.rs API surface drift across 6+ crates
description:
Pass 4 finding (umbrella #982) — DOC_DRIFT, multi-crate.

Crates whose README/lib.rs surface diverges from actual exports:
- ferrotorch (umbrella) README:12 claims '19 optimizers' but prelude exports 4; lib.rs:23 lists 17 submodules, README:60-75 omits profiler/hub/cubecl/mps/xpu/distributed; quick-start example never imports Module despite using it
- ferrotorch-data README:18 claims interop::{load_numpy, load_npz, ArrowDataset} — none exist; actual exports are tensor_to_arrow_array etc.
- ferrotorch-data lib.rs:19-20 + dataloader.rs:31,223,323 leak CL-377/CL-378 tracker IDs into rustdoc
- ferrotorch-train README claims RNG state save/restore; impl does not (overlaps with #1108)
- ferrotorch-distributions lib.rs:13-37 distribution table omits Pareto, Kumaraswamy, VonMises, Weibull
- ferrotorch-hub README example uses unet/maskrcnn/deeplabv3 entries that fail-fast (placeholder SHAs)
- ferrotorch-serialize README signatures don't match actual save_state_dict/load_state_dict shapes
- ferrotorch-profiler README:11 lists OpSummary fields (count, total time, mean, **min**, max) — struct has no min field

Acceptance:
- One PR per crate, OR a single sweep PR that updates README + crate-root rustdoc to match real exports
- Add a doc-drift CI check (e.g., extract pub items, diff against README's example imports).
